steps.dialogue: Let's think about everything you learned in this lesson.

We started this lesson by showing that when fractions have the same denominator, we can compare them by counting the numerators. Then you learned THREE mathematical symbols - [vocab]greater than[/vocab] (>), [vocab]less than[/vocab] (<), and [vocab]equal to[/vocab] (=) - and used them to solve practice problems.
You're seeing WHY we use these symbols to compare fractions. You know that same denominators mean same-size pieces, so comparing becomes as simple as counting.
You've got a method to lean on now: check denominators first, then compare numerators, then choose the right symbol.
You can now compare any fractions with the same denominator. Next time, we'll learn how to compare them when denominators are different.
